title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail, a 2.5-mile path connecting 16 historical sites, attracts over 4 million visitors annually.
Six Flags New England debuts the Quantum Accelerator, New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster, opening April 17.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast runs April 9-18, offering special prix fixe menus at over 30 restaurants.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton and her husband appealed to Rhode Island Supreme Court over noise issues with Warwick neighbors.
Carlton won a 2024 injunction against neighbors to not exceed 60 decibels and stop harassment.
The injunction is challenged by property owners who deny wrongdoing and claim it lacks specificity.

title: Delta is adding winter service between Boston and Rome in 2027
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Delta Air Lines will introduce winter flights between Boston and Rome starting January 2027.
Flights will operate four times weekly until daily service resumes in March 2027.
Fares for the 2027 Boston-Rome flights go on sale April 4, 2026.

title: He’s running his second Boston Marathon for Brigham and Women’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Joe Greeley, 30, from Charlestown, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Brigham and Women's Hospital's Stepping Strong.
Greeley previously ran the 2024 Boston Marathon and found the experience challenging yet rewarding, especially running for a charity.
Inspired by watching the 2025 race, Greeley decided to return, motivated by the charity and camaraderie with friends.

title: ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Everything that happened in Episode 1
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premiered with a 90-minute pilot highlighting local cast members and regional drama.
The episode featured cast members Alicia, Liz, Kelsey, Jo-Ellen, and Rosie, navigating personal and interpersonal challenges.
Tensions include wedding pressure, cannabis business dynamics, unconventional relationships, and conflict at a Goddard Beach picnic.

title: 8 gorgeous places in New England to see daffodils this spring
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: New England Botanic Garden in Boylston showcases 25,000 daffodils during its annual Field of Daffodils event.
Nantucket's 50th annual Community Daffodil Flower Show runs from April 24-26, featuring parades and live performances.
The Meriden Daffodil Festival takes place on May 2-3 in Hubbard Park, Connecticut, with over 600,000 daffodils and live music.

title: Watch the Red Sox Opening Day ceremony honoring 1986 team
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox honor 1986 American League pennant-winning team on 2026 Opening Day at Fenway Park.
Close to 25 players from the 1986 team are attending, including Wade Boggs and Dwight Evans.
The national anthem will be performed by Nik Walker, with no military flyover planned this year.

title: Review & setlist: From flames to twerk-offs, Cardi B commands TD Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cardi B's 'Little Miss Drama' tour sold out at TD Garden, part of a highly successful North American tour.
The concert featured 37 songs, elaborate pyrotechnics, and multiple costume changes across two hours and six acts.
Highlights included a $2,000 'twerk-off' and an arena-wide 'mute challenge', showcasing Cardi B's fan engagement.

title: Emmy winner Amy Poehler spotted at popular Somerville coffee shop
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Amy Poehler visited Diesel Cafe in Somerville, attracting attention as a local celebrity sighting.
Cafe staff member Autumn recognized Poehler, known for 'Parks and Recreation' and 'Saturday Night Live.'
Poehler is a Burlington native and Boston College alum with Emmy and Golden Globe awards.

title: Oscar-winning actress Emma Stone visits North End restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Emma Stone visited Arya Trattoria in Boston's North End, known for its Italian cuisine and meatballs.
The restaurant posted a photo of Stone, a two-time Oscar winner, during her visit on their Instagram.
Notable Arya Trattoria diners include Drake Maye, Dwayne Johnson, and Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla.
